verses.text: 'Comfort ye, comfort ye my people, saith your God'; speak tenderly to Jerusalem, for her warfare is ended and her iniquity pardoned. After judgment comes consolation. God turns from rebuke to the sweet word of comfort and full pardon.

'Comfort ye, comfort ye my people, saith your God'; speak tenderly to Jerusalem, for her warfare is ended and her iniquity pardoned. After judgment comes consolation. God turns from rebuke to the sweet word of comfort and full pardon.

'The voice of him that crieth in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the LORD'; every valley raised, every hill made low, and the glory of the LORD revealed. This is John the Baptist's cry. The way is made ready for the coming of the Lord.

'The voice of him that crieth in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the LORD'; every valley raised, every hill made low, and the glory of the LORD revealed. This is John the Baptist's cry. The way is made ready for the coming of the Lord.

'The voice of him that crieth in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the LORD'; every valley raised, every hill made low, and the glory of the LORD revealed. This is John the Baptist's cry. The way is made ready for the coming of the Lord.
'All flesh is grass,' fading like the flower of the field, 'but the word of our God shall stand for ever.' Man is frail and fleeting. Only God's word endures, a sure ground when all else withers.
'All flesh is grass,' fading like the flower of the field, 'but the word of our God shall stand for ever.' Man is frail and fleeting. Only God's word endures, a sure ground when all else withers.
'All flesh is grass,' fading like the flower of the field, 'but the word of our God shall stand for ever.' Man is frail and fleeting. Only God's word endures, a sure ground when all else withers.

'O Zion... say unto the cities of Judah, Behold your God!' who comes with strength yet 'shall feed his flock like a shepherd... gather the lambs.' Might and tenderness meet. The mighty God is also the gentle Shepherd of His people.

'O Zion... say unto the cities of Judah, Behold your God!' who comes with strength yet 'shall feed his flock like a shepherd... gather the lambs.' Might and tenderness meet. The mighty God is also the gentle Shepherd of His people.

'O Zion... say unto the cities of Judah, Behold your God!' who comes with strength yet 'shall feed his flock like a shepherd... gather the lambs.' Might and tenderness meet. The mighty God is also the gentle Shepherd of His people.
Who measured the seas in His hand, weighed the mountains, or taught the LORD counsel? None. God needs no adviser. His wisdom and power are beyond all comparison or instruction.
Who measured the seas in His hand, weighed the mountains, or taught the LORD counsel? None. God needs no adviser. His wisdom and power are beyond all comparison or instruction.
Who measured the seas in His hand, weighed the mountains, or taught the LORD counsel? None. God needs no adviser. His wisdom and power are beyond all comparison or instruction.
The nations are 'as a drop of a bucket,' the isles as fine dust; all peoples before Him are as nothing. The great powers are tiny to God. What overawes us is less than nothing in His sight.
The nations are 'as a drop of a bucket,' the isles as fine dust; all peoples before Him are as nothing. The great powers are tiny to God. What overawes us is less than nothing in His sight.

To whom then will you liken God? The craftsman casts an idol of metal and wood that cannot move. Idols are absurd before Him. No image men can make can represent the infinite God.

To whom then will you liken God? The craftsman casts an idol of metal and wood that cannot move. Idols are absurd before Him. No image men can make can represent the infinite God.
It is He 'that sitteth upon the circle of the earth,' who stretches the heavens and brings princes to nothing. He rules from on high. The rulers who seem so great He blows away like stubble.
It is He 'that sitteth upon the circle of the earth,' who stretches the heavens and brings princes to nothing. He rules from on high. The rulers who seem so great He blows away like stubble.

It is He 'that sitteth upon the circle of the earth,' who stretches the heavens and brings princes to nothing. He rules from on high. The rulers who seem so great He blows away like stubble.

'To whom then will ye liken me?' Lift up your eyes: He made the stars and 'calleth them all by names,' none missing. He governs the host of heaven. The God who numbers the stars surely watches over you.

'To whom then will ye liken me?' Lift up your eyes: He made the stars and 'calleth them all by names,' none missing. He governs the host of heaven. The God who numbers the stars surely watches over you.

Why complain that your way is hidden from the LORD? He never faints; 'they that wait upon the LORD shall renew their strength... mount up with wings as eagles... run, and not be weary.' He gives strength to the weary. Those who wait on God are carried on tireless wings.
